name: Langley AFB
profile: Military-friendly Langley AFB housing snapshot anchored to Hampton, Newport News, Yorktown, Poquoson, and the Hampton Roads metro, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Stable Hampton Roads military market with strong base-connected demand, coastal insurance sensitivity, and major commute variation by bridge, tunnel, and gate route

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on April 2026 Hampton Roads VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Coastal Virginia ownership cost should include property tax, homeowners insurance, wind/hurricane exposure, flood-zone review, HOA, utilities, commute, and property condition.
Langley buyers should compare Hampton, Newport News, Yorktown, Poquoson, and Chesapeake/Norfolk alternatives by bridge/tunnel exposure, gate route, school zones, insurance, and all-in payment.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate for Hampton Roads / Langley AFB area

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated joint military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_hampton_roads: Safety and property risk vary by neighborhood, flood zone, bridge/tunnel exposure, and commute route. Military families should compare exact address, school zone, gate route, insurance, property condition, and long-term resale logic before committing.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Hampton City Schools, York County, Poquoson, Newport News, and surrounding Hampton Roads districts vary by exact address. Families should verify school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Langley AFB, NASA Langley, Newport News Shipbuilding, Fort Eustis, Norfolk naval demand, contractors, and broader Hampton Roads military turnover.

rental_metrics.notes: Based on 3-bedroom military-family rental planning estimates across Hampton, Newport News, Yorktown, and nearby Langley commute corridors.

special_events: Air Power over Hampton Roads when scheduled
Virginia Air & Space Science Center events
Newport News festivals and shipyard-area events
Norfolk and Virginia Beach regional events

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Major Air Combat Command and joint-base environment with deep Hampton Roads military infrastructure, strong contractor and shipbuilding economy, coastal lifestyle, and broad military-family support.

financial_brief.bah_vs_buy_position: Favorable with coastal and commute discipline
financial_brief.affordability_summary: Langley-area housing is more accessible than many coastal military markets, but flood risk, insurance, commute route, bridge/tunnel exposure, and school-zone variation can change the real monthly answer.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Hampton, Newport News, Yorktown, Poquoson, and selected Peninsula corridors by gate access, insurance, schools, commute, and all-in payment.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Langley AFB gives military families strong Hampton Roads housing access with practical purchase prices, but the best decision depends on gate access, bridge/tunnel exposure, flood risk, insurance, school zones, and commute discipline.

summary_points: Langley AFB sits inside the Hampton Roads military ecosystem with strong Air Force, Army, Navy, NASA, shipbuilding, and contractor demand.
Hampton offers the closest practical housing lane, while Yorktown, Poquoson, Newport News, and Williamsburg-area options create different school, commute, and price tradeoffs.
The market is stable, but flood-zone exposure, coastal insurance, bridge/tunnel patterns, and gate routes materially affect the true housing decision.
Langley works best for buyers who compare exact commute route and school zone before focusing on list price.
The strongest housing choices balance BAH, all-in payment, gate access, flood risk, school quality, and assignment length.
opportunities: Hampton and Newport News offer practical entry points compared with many coastal military markets.
Yorktown and Poquoson may appeal to families prioritizing school-zone and lifestyle fit.
Langley is supported by durable military, NASA, defense-contractor, and shipbuilding demand.
VA buyers can find solid long-term value if they avoid weak-condition homes and flood/insurance surprises.
The Peninsula offers multiple housing lanes without requiring every buyer to cross tunnels daily.
risks: Flood risk and coastal insurance can materially affect affordability.
Bridge and tunnel commutes can create major quality-of-life problems if the wrong corridor is chosen.
School quality and neighborhood fit vary by exact address.
Older homes may need roof, HVAC, crawlspace, moisture, foundation, or drainage review.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale or transaction-cost risk is high.
buyer_notes: Langley buyers should compare Hampton, Newport News, Yorktown, Poquoson, and Williamsburg-area options by gate access, school zones, flood risk, insurance, and all-in payment.
Do not judge commute by mileage alone; gate route, bridge/tunnel exposure, and report time matter.
A slightly higher-priced home in a stronger school/commute/flood profile may be better than a cheaper property with hidden risk.
Homes near water or low-lying areas should be screened for flood zone, elevation, drainage, and insurance cost.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and avoid assuming every Hampton Roads corridor drives the same.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around Langley commute, VA readiness, flood-zone clarity, roof/HVAC condition, school-zone appeal, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes with updated systems, clean moisture/drainage history, and PCS-friendly timing can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to realistic payment logic and commute-specific location value.
Homes in strong Peninsula corridors should emphasize Langley access, school fit, condition, and resale appeal.
investor_angles: Military, NASA, contractor, shipbuilding, student, healthcare, and broader Hampton Roads workforce demand support rental relevance.
Cash flow depends heavily on insurance, taxes, flood risk, maintenance, and acquisition price.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, repairs, flood exposure, roof/HVAC replacement, tenant turnover, and property management.
Clean single-family homes in practical Langley commute corridors may outperform cheaper properties with flood, moisture, or deferred-maintenance risk.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in payment, not just list price, as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, flood zone, commute, gate access, and school quality before locking a neighborhood.
This market rewards disciplined buyers more than emotional stretch buyers.
A slightly longer commute can be worth it if the school zone, flood profile, and property condition are materially stronger.
seller_guidance: Price against current inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have multiple Hampton Roads options.
Military-adjacent demand can be a strength if framed correctly with gate access, condition, and flood/insurance clarity.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Langley AFB and the broader Hampton Roads defense ecosystem.
Investors should still model vacancy, tax drag, insurance, flood exposure, and maintenance conservatively.
A good rental here is about stable spread, clean condition, and strong commute logic — not just headline affordability.

market.market_type_summary: Stable Hampton Roads military market with strong base demand and coastal commute/flood risk overlays

base_profile.market_label: Hampton Roads / Langley Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Fighter Wing / Air Combat Command / Joint Base Support
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 633rd Air Base Wing / Air Combat Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Langley AFB is the Air Force side of Joint Base Langley-Eustis in Hampton, Virginia. The installation supports Air Combat Command, fighter operations, intelligence and mission support functions, joint-base operations, and a large Hampton Roads military ecosystem.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Air combat-focused, coastal, joint-base, commute-sensitive, flood-aware, family-relevant, and highly tied to gate access, BAH fit, bridge/tunnel exposure, school-zone selection, and neighborhood-level screening.
base_profile.base_bluf: Langley is not just a Hampton housing decision. It is a gate-access, commute, flood-zone, insurance, BAH, school-zone, and bridge/tunnel decision. Families should compare nearby areas by realistic gate commute and all-in coastal ownership cost instead of judging only by distance to the base.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize gate access, school-zone verification, flood-zone review, insurance quotes, BAH fit, and commute testing before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, roommate/rental flexibility, and access to food, gyms, waterfront, and daily services.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Langley housing conversations are about gate access, flood/insurance risk, BAH realism, commute route, and family lifestyle fit — not generic Hampton Roads market stats.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Langley is influenced by PCS movement, Air Force permanent party, NASA Langley, defense contractors, Newport News Shipbuilding, Fort Eustis, and broader Hampton Roads military turnover.

best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users wanting to reduce coastal flood/insurance uncertainty
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified

Off-base options in Hampton, Yorktown, Poquoson, Newport News, and surrounding Hampton Roads communities should be compared carefully

on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. The best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, commute tolerance, school priorities, flood/insurance exposure, pet needs, and how long the assignment may last.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Hampton Practical Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Closest practical housing lane for Langley with short commute potential, broad rental options, and strong base relevance.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-20 minutes depending on gate, time of day, flood-prone routes, and exact address
likely_gate_logic: Armistead Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: Neighborhood and property condition vary by exact pocket
Flood and insurance should be checked carefully
Do not choose purely because it is close
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good first-look area for users who value commute simplicity, but exact-address screening is important.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Fox Hill
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Popular Hampton-area corridor with base access, waterfront proximity, and family appeal, but flood and insurance review matter.
best_for: Users wanting Hampton lifestyle near Langley
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on gate, exact address, and traffic
watchouts: Flood zone and insurance should be verified
Older homes may require moisture, crawlspace, and roof inspection
School assignment should be verified by exact address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong Hampton-family candidate if the flood profile, school fit, and property condition are solid.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Yorktown / Tabb
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Family-oriented corridor with strong school/lifestyle appeal and practical access to Langley for many households.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Prices may be higher than Hampton pockets
Commute should be tested during actual duty hours
School zones are address-specific
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong family option when the user has payment room and wants school/lifestyle fit.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Poquoson
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Small coastal city with strong family appeal and proximity to Langley, but flood, insurance, and limited inventory matter.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-30 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and flood/traffic conditions
likely_gate_logic: King Street Gate depending on route
watchouts: Flood risk and insurance can be significant
Inventory may be limited
Prices can stretch some BAH profiles
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Lifestyle-first family option when flood/insurance math and payment still work.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Newport News / Kiln Creek
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Broad housing corridor with services, shopping, and commute options, but neighborhood and traffic vary heavily.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-40 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Neighborhood quality varies significantly
Traffic on major corridors should be tested
Property condition and school zones require exact-address review
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good practical option when the exact neighborhood and commute route are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Williamsburg Extended Commute
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Higher-lifestyle and family-oriented extended commute option, but distance and traffic must be accepted upfront.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 45-70+ minutes depending on exact address, route, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Long commute can become a daily burden
Higher prices may stretch BAH
Only makes sense for specific lifestyle or family reasons
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Lifestyle-first option, not commute-first. Best for families who intentionally accept the drive.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Norfolk / Virginia Beach Extended Cross-Water Options
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Large lifestyle and employment corridor across the water, but bridge/tunnel dependence can make Langley commute risky.
best_for: Users with spouse employment on the Southside
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 45-90+ minutes depending on bridge/tunnel conditions, route, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Bridge/tunnel traffic can be unpredictable
Not recommended for most Langley-first households
Commute stress can overpower lifestyle benefits
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Use carefully. This is only logical when household employment or lifestyle needs justify cross-water commute exposure.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Langley, gate access and bridge/tunnel exposure matter more than straight-line distance. A home can look close on a map but become difficult if the route depends on limited-hour gates, flood-prone roads, or Hampton Roads choke points.
primary_commute_factors: Hampton Roads Bridge-Tunnel and Monitor-Merrimac exposure if living off-Peninsula
Exact duty location on or near the installation
Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, flood, and safety screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Gate choice and flood-prone roads can still change the real commute.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for families wanting stronger schools, larger homes, or specific lifestyle corridors
commute_bands.watchout: Can become painful for early reporting, shift work, or frequent base errands.

commute_bands.label: Bridge/tunnel risk
commute_bands.fit: Only for users with strong household reasons to live off-Peninsula
commute_bands.watchout: Bridge and tunnel congestion can destroy commute reliability.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Check limited-hour gates only as optional commute helpers.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, users should test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window they would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Langley-area housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, insurance, flood risk, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH can support ownership in parts of Hampton and Newport News, but Yorktown, Poquoson, and waterfront areas may require stronger income or tighter debt control.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter, not a promise of affordability. The Base tab should push users into the PCSUnited financial calculator when the housing gap, insurance cost, or flood exposure looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Langley should verify school zones, flood risk, childcare options, medical access, commute reality, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Common Langley-area school district research may include Hampton City Schools, York County School Division, Poquoson City Public Schools, Newport News Public Schools, and other address-specific districts depending on the neighborhood.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources before relying on any single facility.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: Hampton Roads can offer broad spouse employment across military, healthcare, shipbuilding, education, tourism, and government sectors, but commute and bridge/tunnel exposure should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Control Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, insurance, flood premiums, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check pet policies for on-base housing, rentals, and temporary lodging.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
Avoid choosing purely by distance or rent price.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, flood risk, insurance, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.
Review whether the selected area supports the user's assignment length and family needs.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official JBLE gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the same as commute
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access, flood-prone roads, and traffic timing.

pcs_watchouts.title: Bridge and tunnel exposure matters
pcs_watchouts.message: Living off-Peninsula may add unpredictable Hampton Roads Bridge-Tunnel or Monitor-Merrimac commute risk.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, insurance, flood risk, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Flood risk can change the monthly answer
pcs_watchouts.message: Flood zone, elevation, drainage, and insurance should be checked before treating a property as affordable.

pcs_watchouts.title: Limited-hour gates can distort commute estimates
pcs_watchouts.message: A route that works during the morning may not work later if the preferred gate closes.

pcs_watchouts.title: On-base housing is not automatically the best financial answer
pcs_watchouts.message: Compare waitlist, BAH capture, commute, home condition, schools, flood risk, and lifestyle before deciding.

buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Lead with BAH realism, monthly payment clarity, flood/insurance awareness, commute testing, and VA loan education.

buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Keep recommendations practical: commute, rent, safety, flood exposure, property condition, and lease flexibility.

likely_questions: Should I buy if I may be here longer?
Where can I balance family and commute?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Use assignment length, family needs, flood/insurance profile, and rent-vs-buy math to guide the recommendation.

Easy access to Hampton, Yorktown, Newport News, and NASA Langley
common_objections: Affordability gap between BAH and mortgage
recommended_agent_behavior: Always recommend school-zone verification by address.
Use payment math, not just listing price.
Discuss gate access and flood risk early.
Compare on-base housing as a legitimate alternative.
Avoid generic Hampton Roads advice when the user is assigned to Langley.

source_notes.label: Air Force Housing - Joint Base Langley-Eustis
Military OneSource - Joint Base Langley-Eustis housing

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, flood zones, insurance estimates, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Hampton Roads regional planning estimates

rent.provider: Local rental listing estimates / Hampton Roads rental observations

parser_notes: This Langley AFB file follows the same tempo and structure as the uploaded Lackland.json reference, including market metrics, costs, ownership notes, rental metrics, bedroom bands, housing market object, base_profile, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, family readiness, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and compatibility notes.
Base-facing label is set to Langley AFB, while Hampton Roads is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.
This file includes the shared placeholder base_image_url requested for bases without finished map images.
Gate and housing office details are aligned to official JBLE and Air Force Housing public sources as of June 27, 2026.
